description: Build a CLI tool that lets users query the Cassini mission SQLite database using natural language. TypeScript + Bun, using @anthropic-ai/sdk to translate natural language questions into SQL, then executing the SQL against the local SQLite database via bun:sqlite. The CLI lives in a new cli/ directory with its own package.json. The primary UX is: user asks a question in plain English, Claude generates the SQL, the CLI runs it and displays formatted results. Also supports direct SQL, shortcut commands, and an interactive REPL. Requires ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env var.

tasks.title: Create cli/ package scaffold and entry point
tasks.description:
Create the cli/ directory with package.json, tsconfig.json, and src/index.ts entry point.

package.json:
- name: cassini-cli
- dependencies: @anthropic-ai/sdk
- devDependencies: @types/bun, typescript
- scripts: { "dev": "bun src/index.ts", "build": "bun build src/index.ts --compile --outfile=dist/cassini" }

tsconfig.json: same pattern as mcp/ (ESNext, bundler resolution, bun-types).

src/index.ts:
- Parse argv using a simple hand-rolled parser (no deps like commander/yargs)
- Support these subcommands: ask, query, tables, schema, repl
- The default (no subcommand but with positional args) should behave like 'ask' — i.e. 'cassini what moons did we observe' is equivalent to 'cassini ask what moons did we observe'
- Support flags: --db <path> (default: ./cassini.db), --limit <n> (default: 50), --format <table|json|csv> (default: table), --help, --sql (show generated SQL without running it)
- If no subcommand and no args, print usage and exit
- Import db functions from a local db.ts (copy/adapt from mcp/src/db.ts — do NOT import across packages)

The entry point should dispatch to handler functions (implemented in later tasks) — for now, stub them with console.log placeholders.
acceptanceCriteria: cli/package.json exists with @anthropic-ai/sdk as dependency
cli/tsconfig.json exists
cli/src/index.ts parses subcommands and flags from argv
Running 'bun cli/src/index.ts --help' prints usage info
Running 'bun cli/src/index.ts' (no args) prints usage and exits
Bare positional args (no subcommand) are treated as 'ask'
bun install in cli/ succeeds

tasks.title: Create cli/src/db.ts — database module adapted from mcp
tasks.description:
Copy and adapt mcp/src/db.ts into cli/src/db.ts. The core logic is identical — openDb, getSchema, query — but the query function should accept a limit parameter instead of hardcoding 100.

Changes from mcp version:
- query(db, sql, limit?) — limit parameter defaults to 50, caller can override. Still enforces the write-pattern guard.
- Add a new function: queryRaw(db, sql, limit?) — returns the parsed row objects (not JSON string) for use by the formatter. This is the primary function the CLI will use.
- getTableList(db) — returns array of { name: string, count: number } for the tables command.

Keep openDb and getSchema as-is. Import from 'bun:sqlite'.
acceptanceCriteria: cli/src/db.ts exports openDb, getSchema, query, queryRaw, getTableList
queryRaw returns an array of row objects, not a JSON string
queryRaw respects the limit parameter
Write statements are rejected with an error
getTableList returns [{name, count}] for each table

tasks.title: Create cli/src/formatter.ts — table, JSON, and CSV output
tasks.description:
Create a formatter module that takes an array of row objects and outputs them in one of three formats.

Export a single function: formatRows(rows: Record<string, unknown>[], format: 'table' | 'json' | 'csv'): string

Table format (default):
- Auto-detect column widths from header + data (cap each column at 40 chars, truncate with '…')
- Print header row with column names
- Print a separator line of dashes
- Print each row
- Print a footer: '(N rows)'
- Align columns with 2-space gaps
- If no rows, print '(no results)'

JSON format:
- JSON.stringify(rows, null, 2)

CSV format:
- Header row with column names
- Data rows with proper quoting (quote fields containing commas, quotes, or newlines; escape inner quotes by doubling)

All formats should handle empty results gracefully.
acceptanceCriteria: cli/src/formatter.ts exports formatRows function
Table format shows aligned columns with header, separator, and row count footer
JSON format outputs pretty-printed JSON array
CSV format produces valid CSV with proper quoting
Empty results produce '(no results)' for table, '[]' for JSON, empty for CSV
Long values are truncated at 40 chars in table format

tasks.title: Create cli/src/ai.ts — natural language to SQL via Anthropic SDK
tasks.description:
Create the AI module that translates natural language questions into SQL using the Anthropic SDK.

Export: async function naturalLanguageToSql(question: string, schema: string): Promise<{ sql: string, explanation: string }>

Implementation:
- Import Anthropic from '@anthropic-ai/sdk' (the SDK auto-reads ANTHROPIC_API_KEY from env)
- Use claude-sonnet-4-20250514 model (fast, cheap, good at SQL)
- System prompt should include:
  - The full database schema (passed in as parameter)
  - Instructions: generate a single read-only SELECT statement, no mutations
  - The database is SQLite — use SQLite syntax
  - Respond with JSON: { "sql": "...", "explanation": "one sentence explaining what the query does" }
  - If the question is ambiguous, make reasonable assumptions and note them in the explanation
  - Use LIKE with % wildcards for fuzzy text matching
  - Default to reasonable LIMITs if the query could return many rows
- Parse the response JSON, extract sql and explanation
- If the response can't be parsed, throw a clear error

Also export: function validateApiKey(): boolean — checks if ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is set, prints a helpful error message if not.

IMPORTANT: The Anthropic SDK constructor takes no args — it reads ANTHROPIC_API_KEY from the environment automatically. Do NOT pass apiKey to the constructor.
acceptanceCriteria: cli/src/ai.ts exports naturalLanguageToSql and validateApiKey
naturalLanguageToSql calls the Anthropic API with the schema in the system prompt
Response is parsed as JSON with sql and explanation fields
Uses claude-sonnet-4-20250514 model
validateApiKey returns false and prints error if ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is missing
Errors from the API are caught and rethrown with clear messages

tasks.title: Implement all CLI command handlers
tasks.description:
Create cli/src/commands.ts with handler functions for each subcommand. Each handler receives the parsed options (db path, limit, format) and any positional args.

Commands:

1. ask <natural language question> — The primary command. Calls naturalLanguageToSql to get SQL, prints the explanation and generated SQL (dimmed), runs the query, formats and prints results. If --sql flag is set, only print the generated SQL without executing.
   Example: cassini ask "what are the most common observation targets?"
   Output:
     Querying: SELECT target, COUNT(*) as count FROM master_plan GROUP BY target ORDER BY count DESC LIMIT 20
     (then the formatted table)

2. query <sql> — Run arbitrary SQL directly (no AI). Pass through to queryRaw, format output.
   Example: cassini query "SELECT target, COUNT(*) as n FROM master_plan GROUP BY target ORDER BY n DESC LIMIT 10"

3. tables — List all tables with row counts. Use getTableList, format as table.

4. schema — Print full schema. Use getSchema, print as-is (not formatted as table).

All commands should:
- Open the DB, run the query, format the output, print to stdout
- Handle errors with a clean message to stderr + exit code 1
- Respect the --format and --limit flags
- The 'ask' command should check validateApiKey() before calling the API
acceptanceCriteria: cli/src/commands.ts exports handler functions for all 4 commands
ask command translates natural language to SQL via AI and runs it
ask command prints the generated SQL before results
ask command with --sql flag prints SQL only without executing
query command runs arbitrary SQL and formats output
tables command shows table names and row counts
schema command prints the database schema
All commands respect --format and --limit flags
Errors print to stderr and exit with code 1
ask command validates API key before making API call

tasks.title: Implement interactive REPL mode
tasks.description:
Create cli/src/repl.ts with an interactive REPL for running queries.

Behavior:
- Print a welcome banner: 'Cassini DB — interactive mode. Ask questions in English or type SQL directly.'
- Prompt: 'cassini> '
- Input handling: if the input starts with SELECT/PRAGMA/EXPLAIN/WITH (case-insensitive), treat as raw SQL. Otherwise, treat as natural language and run through the AI pipeline.
- For natural language queries, print the generated SQL (dimmed) before the results.
- Special dot-commands:
  - .help — print available commands
  - .tables — same as tables command
  - .schema — same as schema command
  - .format <table|json|csv> — switch output format
  - .limit <n> — change row limit
  - .sql — toggle showing/hiding generated SQL
  - .quit or .exit — exit
- Ctrl+C / Ctrl+D exits cleanly
- Use Bun's built-in readline or process.stdin line-by-line reading (no external deps)
- Handle errors gracefully — print the error and continue (don't crash)
- Validate API key on entry — warn if missing but still allow raw SQL

Wire this up in src/index.ts so 'cassini repl' enters this mode.
acceptanceCriteria: cli/src/repl.ts exports a startRepl function
REPL prints a welcome banner and prompt
Natural language input is translated to SQL via AI and results displayed
SQL input (starting with SELECT etc.) is executed directly
Generated SQL is printed before results for natural language queries
All dot-commands work (.help, .tables, .schema, .format, .limit, .sql, .quit, .exit)
.format switches the output format for subsequent queries
Errors are caught and printed without crashing the REPL
Ctrl+C and Ctrl+D exit cleanly
Warns if ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is missing but allows raw SQL usage

tasks.title: Wire everything together and test end-to-end
tasks.description:
Final integration: update cli/src/index.ts to import and call the real command handlers from commands.ts and repl.ts (replacing any stubs). Ensure the full pipeline works end-to-end.

Verify all these work:
- bun cli/src/index.ts --help
- bun cli/src/index.ts tables
- bun cli/src/index.ts schema
- bun cli/src/index.ts query "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM master_plan"
- bun cli/src/index.ts ask "how many observations are there?"
- bun cli/src/index.ts ask "what moons were observed?" --format json
- bun cli/src/index.ts ask "show me all Titan flybys" --sql
- bun cli/src/index.ts "what teams have the most observations?" (bare ask, no subcommand)
- bun cli/src/index.ts query "SELECT * FROM master_plan LIMIT 3" --format csv
- bun cli/src/index.ts query "SELECT target, COUNT(*) as n FROM master_plan GROUP BY target ORDER BY n DESC" --limit 5

Also add the shebang #!/usr/bin/env bun to src/index.ts so the compiled binary works.

Update the build script to verify the compiled binary works: bun build src/index.ts --compile --outfile=dist/cassini
acceptanceCriteria: All command handlers are imported and wired in src/index.ts
All 10 example commands listed above produce correct output
Natural language queries return relevant results
Generated SQL is shown before results for 'ask' commands
--sql flag shows SQL without executing
Bare positional args work as implicit 'ask'
Table format is aligned and readable
JSON format is valid JSON
CSV format is valid CSV
--limit flag restricts result count
--format flag switches output format
--db flag allows specifying a custom database path
src/index.ts has #!/usr/bin/env bun shebang
bun build cli/src/index.ts --compile --outfile=cli/dist/cassini succeeds
